repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue proposal states this exact two-decision g-formula lemma; closest Stat entries stat_dose_response_minimax and stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap concern different estimands.

published_prior_art.cite: LuedtkeSofryginVanderLaanCarone2017 develops longitudinal g-formula sequential-regression estimators and sequential double robustness.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue Stat entry gives the direct-backward transport decomposition for this transition-composed model.

published_prior_art.cite: LuedtkeSofryginVanderLaanCarone2017 analyzes first-order longitudinal g-formula remainders; the displayed direct-backward transport decomposition is a model-specific algebraic reformulation.

repo_prior_art.cite: No other in-catalogue Stat kernel states a matched minimax frontier for a two-period transition-composed g-formula; stat_dose_response_minimax and stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap use different causal functionals and classes.

published_prior_art.cite: BonviniKennedyKeele2025 publicly reports two-time-point g-formula HOIF estimators with minimax-optimal rates in some anisotropic/additive models, while the available abstract does not state or subsume the proposed incoming-state C^t(L1) transition class, direct-backward construction, or two-channel rate.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue proposal characterizes scalar versus multi-index correction order for a sequential transport chain.

published_prior_art.cite: RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2016TR applies HOIF methodology to time-dependent treatment, but does not state a necessary-and-sufficient finite multi-index correction criterion for this direct-backward chain.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue Stat entry uses a finite exact-transition-smoothness subclass to separate longitudinal rate channels.

published_prior_art.cite: The exact-Hölder-member construction is standard analysis; its implication for the proposed transition channel is conditional on the new frontier.

repo_prior_art.cite: No duplicate in the in-catalogue Stat entries, although it is a standard reduction rather than a new kernel.

published_prior_art.cite: RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2016TR supplies the relevant HOIF missing-response/time-dependent-treatment benchmark after the first transition channel is removed.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:two-channel-minimax-frontier and dependent statements
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The proposal is conditional on an exactly state-sufficient, two-period observed-law class with strict overlap, fixed smoothness indices, and iid trajectories; no empirical validation of state sufficiency is claimed.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any claimed estimator rate, minimax elbow, separation result, and correction-order result holds only over the stated conditional two-period Markov/state-sufficient model, not under approximate state sufficiency, unknown smoothness, or longer horizons.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add an assumption-by-assumption comparison table against BonviniKennedyKeele2025, anchored to its abstract and any subsequently available manuscript, stating whether each anisotropic/additive class contains, is contained in, or is incomparable with the incoming-state C^t(L1) class.
Add an approximate-state-sufficiency extension with an explicit residual-history bias term, so the result remains informative when H2 is only an approximately sufficient state.
State an adaptive version over unknown (alpha_1,alpha_2,beta,t), or prove an impossibility/adaptation penalty that sharpens the practical meaning of the elbow.

recommended_next_step: Advance to Stage 0 with the conditional two-period frontier, while preserving the Bonvini comparison and state-sufficiency limitation as explicit scope.
